content.text: Cluster is at v24.1.0, partition=2. I'll do a controlled rolling update one node at a time, starting by updating the image (partition=2 means only pod-2 updates first).

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb set image sts/crdb-cluster crdb-cluster=cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.1
echo "Image set. Waiting for pod-2 to update..."
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  img=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pod crdb-cluster-2 -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].image}' 2>/dev/null)
  ready=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pod crdb-cluster-2 -o jsonpath='{.status.containerStatuses[0].ready}' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ "$img" = "cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.1" ] && [ "$ready" = "true" ]; then echo "pod-2 updated and ready"; break; fi
  sleep 5
done
kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods

content.text: Rollout complete. Node 1 (pod-0) shows not-live since it just restarted 73s ago. Let me re-confirm liveness has settled.

input.command:
until kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node status --insecure 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR>1{print $(NF-1),$NF}' | grep -qc false; do :; done 2>/dev/null
# Simpler: just poll until no false in last two columns
until ! kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node status --insecure 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR>1{print $(NF-1),$NF}' | grep -q false; do sleep 3; done
echo "=== All nodes live ==="; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node status --insecure 2>&1
